title: Walking, Step by Step
intention: To walk slowly and stay awake to each step, the ground, and the air on the skin.

lines.text: Welcome. Let's walk together, slowly, with our eyes open.
Come to a stop for a moment. Stand still and let your weight settle down through your feet.
Let your eyes stay soft and open. You don't need to stare at anything. Just let the world be there.
Take one easy breath. Feel the ground holding you up.
Now begin to walk, slower than usual. There's no place to get to.
Feel one foot lift, swing forward, and meet the ground.
Notice the heel touching first, then the whole sole, then the toes.
Let the other foot do the same. Lift. Forward. Down.
Keep your attention right where the foot meets the ground.
When the mind drifts, bring it back to the next step.
Feel the simple rhythm. One foot, then the other.
Now notice the air on your skin. On your face and your hands.
Maybe it's warm, maybe cool. Just feel it as you move through it.
Let your breath move on its own. You don't have to steer it.
Step. Breath. Air on the skin. All of it happening together.
If a sound reaches you, let it come and go. Keep walking.
Feel how steady the ground is, meeting each foot in turn.
Stay with a few more steps, slow and plain.
Now let your pace return to something natural and easy.
Keep a little of this attention with you. The feet, the ground, the air.
Wherever you go next, you can walk like this. One step at a time.
Carry on now, awake and easy on your feet.
